content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture:

1. The current server cert expires in 2 days (Jun 23 - Jun 25, 2026)
2. MongoDB pods mount `/etc/mongo-cert/server.pem` from secret `mongodb-tls-cert`, and `/etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt` from `mongodb-tls-ca`
3. The cert has SANs for: localhost, mongo, mongo.mongodb, mongo.mongodb.svc, mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local, mongo-rs-0/1/2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
4. The `server.pem` file bundles both the certificate and private key, while the CA credentials live separately in the `mongodb-tls-ca` secret

My approach is to extract the CA cert and key, generate a new server key and certificate signing request using the openssl toolbox, sign it with the CA to create a cert valid for about a year, merge the new cert and key into `server.pem`, and update the secret so the pods automatically pick up the changes.

But here's the critical issue: Kubernetes will eventually refresh the mounted files, but that takes a minute or two. More importantly, MongoDB doesn't hot-reload TLS certificates—it needs to be restarted to use the new cert. So I'll need to do a rolling restart of the StatefulSet pods one at a time to maintain availability while the new certificate takes effect. I'll handle the certificate generation and signing operations inside the openssl-toolbox pod where OpenSSL is available, then proceed with the rolling restart.

content.text: The current cert expires in 2 days (Jun 25). I'll generate a new 1-year cert using the openssl-toolbox pod with the same SANs, update the secret, and rolling-restart the StatefulSet for zero downtime.

input.command:
# Extract CA cert and key into the openssl-toolbox pod
CA_CRT=$(kubectl get secret mongodb-tls-ca -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' | base64 -d)
CA_KEY=$(kubectl get secret mongodb-tls-ca -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.key}' | base64 -d)

kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
cat > /tmp/ca.crt <<'CACERT'
${CA_CRT}
CACERT
cat > /tmp/ca.key <<'CAKEY'
${CA_KEY}
CAKEY
echo 'CA files written'
ls -la /tmp/ca.*
"
input.description: Write CA cert and key into openssl-toolbox pod

content.content:
CA files written
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root          1111 Jun 23 13:42 /tmp/ca.crt
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root          1704 Jun 23 13:42 /tmp/ca.key

tool_use_result.stdout:
CA files written
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root          1111 Jun 23 13:42 /tmp/ca.crt
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root          1704 Jun 23 13:42 /tmp/ca.key

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
# Generate new server private key
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/server.key 2048

# Create CSR with all required SANs
cat > /tmp/server.cnf <<'EOF'
[req]
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
req_extensions = v3_req
prompt = no

[req_distinguished_name]
CN = mongo-rs

[v3_req]
keyUsage = keyEncipherment, dataEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth, clientAuth
subjectAltName = @alt_names

[alt_names]
DNS.1 = localhost
DNS.2 = mongo
DNS.3 = mongo.mongodb
DNS.4 = mongo.mongodb.svc
DNS.5 = mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.6 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.7 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.8 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
EOF

openssl req -new -key /tmp/server.key -out /tmp/server.csr -config /tmp/server.cnf
echo 'CSR generated'

# Sign the CSR with CA, valid for 365 days
cat > /tmp/server_ext.cnf <<'EOF'
authorityKeyIdentifier=keyid,issuer
basicConstraints=CA:FALSE
keyUsage = digitalSignature, keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth, clientAuth
subjectAltName = @alt_names

[alt_names]
DNS.1 = localhost
DNS.2 = mongo
DNS.3 = mongo.mongodb
DNS.4 = mongo.mongodb.svc
DNS.5 = mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.6 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.7 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.8 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
EOF

openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/server.csr \
  -CA /tmp/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/server.crt \
  -days 365 \
  -extensions SAN \
  -extfile /tmp/server_ext.cnf

echo 'Certificate signed'
openssl x509 -noout -text -in /tmp/server.crt | grep -E '(Not Before|Not After|Subject Alternative)'
"
input.description: Generate new server key, CSR, and sign with CA for 365 days

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
cat > /tmp/server_ext.cnf <<'EOF'
authorityKeyIdentifier=keyid,issuer
basicConstraints=CA:FALSE
keyUsage = digitalSignature, keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth, clientAuth
subjectAltName = DNS:localhost,DNS:mongo,DNS:mongo.mongodb,DNS:mongo.mongodb.svc,DNS:mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local,DNS:mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local,DNS:mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local,DNS:mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
EOF

openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/server.csr \
  -CA /tmp/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/server.crt \
  -days 365 \
  -extfile /tmp/server_ext.cnf

echo 'Exit code:' \$?
openssl x509 -noout -text -in /tmp/server.crt | grep -E '(Not Before|Not After|Subject Alternative|DNS:)'
"

content.text: New cert is valid until Jun 23 2027. Now combine cert+key into `server.pem` and update the secret.

input.command:
# Concatenate cert and key into server.pem (MongoDB requires both in one file)
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
cat /tmp/server.crt /tmp/server.key > /tmp/server.pem
echo 'server.pem created, size:' \$(wc -c < /tmp/server.pem)
"
input.description: Concatenate cert and key into server.pem

input.command:
# Read the new server.pem from the pod and update the secret
NEW_PEM=$(kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/server.pem | base64 -w 0)
echo "PEM base64 length: ${#NEW_PEM}"

kubectl patch secret mongodb-tls-cert -n mongodb \
  --type='json' \
  -p="[{\"op\":\"replace\",\"path\":\"/data/server.pem\",\"value\":\"${NEW_PEM}\"}]"
input.description: Update mongodb-tls-cert secret with new server.pem

content.text: Secret updated. Now perform a rolling restart of the StatefulSet — secondaries first, then primary — to pick up the new cert with zero downtime.

input.description: Rolling restart MongoDB StatefulSet to load new cert

input.command:
# Poll rollout status until complete
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl rollout status statefulset/mongo-rs -n mongodb --timeout=10s 2>&1)
  echo "[${i}] ${STATUS}"
  if echo "${STATUS}" | grep -q "successfully rolled out"; then
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Wait for rolling restart to complete

description: Wait for rolling restart to complete
